description: The adjective naming duration of existence, the quality of having existed for extended time. In Phi, oldness is observed with respect—what has endured has proved its value, has accumulated wisdom, has weathered time's test. This word names temporal extent without decay's stigma, honoring age as achievement rather than decline.
sound_symbolism: The word opens with 'se', where the sibilant 's' creates the sound of time passing—the hiss of years flowing. The clear 'e' brings awareness to duration. This flows through 'ra', where the rolling 'r' captures time's continuous unfolding, years accumulating. The final 'o' opens into depth, the fullness that age brings. Three syllables give appropriate weight to accumulated time.
grammatical_notes: Functions as an adjective preceding nouns: 'serao kerou' (old smonae). Can be used predicatively. Distinguished from 'newu' (new) as temporal opposite, with positive rather than negative connotation of duration.

pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Traditional cultures revered age—in people, in trees, in places, in traditions. 'Serao' preserves this honoring of duration as source of wisdom and stability.
pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: What has endured reveals truth about what can last. 'Serao' names the quality of time-tested existence while remaining aware that all things eventually pass.
